eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Myntra shows subsidized liquidation, Meesho shows price-driven volume. Demand quality is inconsistent.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra strong (discount-driven)
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present at full price.
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank and reviews at 65% off, but discount is the demand driver.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is there genuine style demand, or is the Myntra signal entirely discount-driven?
conflicts.proves: The style has no premium traction and mass retail demand is price-sensitive.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has sustainable demand at any price point.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount context vs Meesho volume
conflicts.myntra_says: Subsidized Liquidation: discount IS demand, low rating (3.2).
conflicts.buyer_question: Does Meesho volume represent genuine value-fashion demand or is it also discount-driven?
conflicts.proves: Meesho shows real volume at low price, but Myntra's deep discount suggests weak style preference.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether Meesho buyers would pay more or if style has lasting appeal.

convergences.title: Demand exists at low price points
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (discounted) and Meesho (low price) show sales volume, indicating some demand at low price.
convergences.proves: The style can sell when priced aggressively.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at higher margins or without heavy discount.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume (8000+ units) at ₹299 with moderate rating, suggesting price-sensitive demand exists. If sourced at low cost, there may be a margin opportunity in value-fashion channels.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: Nykaa absence indicates no premium demand. Myntra signal is heavily distorted by 65% discount and low rating (3.2), meaning demand is price-driven, not style-driven. Reseller growth on Meesho is not accelerating, limiting trend momentum.

nykaa.reason: No data; absence is a signal but not actionable.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: Evidence is discordant: Nykaa absent, Myntra signal is discount-driven, Meesho shows volume but at low price. No clean convergence to justify capital commitment.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Would Meesho volume persist at a higher price point (e.g., ₹499) without heavy discount?

convergences.proves: The style has appeal in premium and mass retail channels at full or near-full price

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong premium demand at full price with high ratings, and Myntra confirms moderate demand at low discount. This suggests the style has genuine appeal among quality-conscious buyers willing to pay a premium.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence indicates no demand in price-sensitive markets, which may limit total addressable market. The trend appears confined to premium segments, and without internal POS data, validation in your own stores is missing.

eval-mass-only.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho shows strong price-driven demand at ₹199, but Nykaa absent and Myntra moderate with discount risk — demand quality is uncertain.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Meesho strong: premium vs value demand gap

conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at discount (35% off), but unclear if style-driven.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at ₹199 with reseller growth.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a price-only commodity or does it have style appeal that can sustain at higher price points?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists at low price points; premium channel shows zero interest.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether demand is sustainable at higher margins or if it's purely price-driven.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount dependency vs Meesho pure price sensitivity
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at 35% discount with unclear driver; velocity down-weighted 25%.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at ₹199 with no discount mentioned (likely low margin).
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the Myntra demand real or propped up by discount? Would it hold at full price?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists but is sensitive to price; discount may be necessary to move units.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove that demand is style-driven or that it would persist without discount.

convergences.title: Demand exists across mass channels
convergences.detail: Both Myntra and Meesho show sales activity, indicating some level of demand for basic cotton kurtis.
convergences.proves: There is a market for this product at low to moderate price points.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the demand is for this specific style or that it can command higher prices.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume (52,000+ units) with accelerating reseller growth, indicating viral potential in value-fashion. If the style has any unique appeal, it could scale quickly at low price points.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence and Myntra's discount dependency suggest demand is purely price-driven. Low ratings on Meesho (3.4★) and Myntra (3.5★) indicate quality or style issues. No premium demand signal exists.

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: strong clean demand across premium and mass channels with no discount distortion

convergences.title: Strong presence across all external sources
convergences.detail: Nykaa (strong, low discount), Myntra (strong, low discount, high rank), Meesho (strong, high volume, reseller growth) all indicate robust demand for golden kurtis.
convergences.proves: The trend is real and spans premium to value segments without reliance on heavy discounts.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the demand is sustainable long-term or if it will cannibalize other styles.

convergences.title: Low discount distortion across channels
convergences.detail: Nykaa at 5% off, Myntra at 10% off (Strategic Value Pricing), Meesho at likely low discount given volume. No suspect or liquidation discounts detected.
convergences.proves: Demand is genuine and not artificially propped up by markdowns.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the trend is seasonal or has staying power beyond current season.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden kurtis show strong, clean demand across premium (Nykaa), mass retail (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels with minimal discount reliance. Myntra's high rank and low discount indicate style-driven purchasing, while Meesho's reseller growth suggests an emerging trend in tier-2/3/4 markets.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data exists to validate external signals. The price gap between Nykaa (₹1450) and Meesho (₹349) may indicate different customer segments, but this is not a conflict—it's a range. Without internal sales data, the true demand at your price point remains unconfirmed.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high rating, but limited product count (2).

myntra.reason: Strong presence, high rank, low discount, high review count, no noise flags.

meesho.reason: Strong presence, high volume, reseller growth accelerating, good rating.

eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: Will the demand hold at your specific price point and margin structure without internal validation?

eval-silent-meesho.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand strong (Nykaa) and mass retail moderate (Myntra), but Meesho absent — demand may be limited to higher price bands

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho absent in value segment
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong demand at ₹2200 with zero discount, high ratings
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at 20% discount, clean signal
conflicts.meesho_says: No demand at all in price-sensitive markets
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this kurti a premium-only trend that won't scale to value-fashion customers?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists at premium price points with low discount sensitivity
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether demand exists at lower price points or in tier-2/3 markets

convergences.title: Clean demand signal at premium to mid price points
convergences.detail: Nykaa shows zero-discount demand at ₹2200; Myntra shows strategic pricing at 20% off with good ratings and velocity. Both indicate genuine style preference, not discount-driven.
convergences.proves: The style has appeal among quality-conscious buyers willing to pay ₹1,500-2,200
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this appeal extends to price-sensitive or value-fashion buyers

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: The niche kurti shows strong premium demand with zero discount on Nykaa and clean strategic pricing on Myntra, indicating genuine style preference. If this trend trickles down to value segments, early positioning could capture a growing market.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho absence suggests no current demand in tier-2/3 markets, which may limit scalability. The premium-only nature could mean a smaller total addressable market. Without internal POS data, ground truth is unknown.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, zero discount, high ratings, sufficient reviews

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, clean discount context, good velocity and ratings

internal.reason: No prior data — neutral, not a conflict

eval-silent-meesho.bet_rationale: Premium demand is confirmed but value segment is absent, creating uncertainty about scalability. A small trial in premium-focused stores can validate real sell-through without overcommitting.
eval-silent-meesho.key_uncertainty: Will the style gain traction in value-fashion channels (Meesho) or remain a premium niche?
